question_html: Find the inverse of the matrix (if it exists) given {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 3&3&0 \\ 5&2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 3&3&0 \\ 5&2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 3&3&0 \\ 5&2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(-3 - 0) - 0 + 0 {tex} = - 3 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists. {tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&0 \\ 2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 3 - 0} \right) = - 3,{/tex} {tex}{A_{12}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&0 \\ 5&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 3 - 0} \right) = 3{/tex} {tex}{A_{13}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&3 \\ 5&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {6 - 15} \right) = - 9,{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}{A_{22}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 5&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 1 - 0} \right) = - 1,{/tex} {tex}{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 5&2 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {2 - 0} \right) = - 2{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 3&0 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0,{/tex} {tex}{A_{32}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 3&0 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}{A_{33}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 3&3 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {3 - 0} \right) = 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&3&{ - 9} \\ 0&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&0&3 \end{array}} \right]' {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&0&0 \\ 3&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A = \frac{{ - 1}}{3} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&0&0 \\ 3&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 3&3&0 \\ 5&2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 3&3&0 \\ 5&2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(-3 - 0) - 0 + 0 {tex} = - 3 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists.

{tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&0 \\ 2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 3 - 0} \right) = - 3,{/tex} {tex}{A_{12}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&0 \\ 5&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 3 - 0} \right) = 3{/tex}

{tex}{A_{13}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&3 \\ 5&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {6 - 15} \right) = - 9,{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 2&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}{A_{22}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 5&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 1 - 0} \right) = - 1,{/tex} {tex}{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 5&2 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {2 - 0} \right) = - 2{/tex}

{tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 3&0 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0,{/tex} {tex}{A_{32}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 3&0 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 0} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}{A_{33}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 3&3 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {3 - 0} \right) = 3{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&3&{ - 9} \\ 0&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&0&3 \end{array}} \right]' {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&0&0 \\ 3&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A = \frac{{ - 1}}{3} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&0&0 \\ 3&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_html: Find the inverse of the matrix (if it exists) given {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&3 \\ 4&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 7}&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&3 \\ 4&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 7}&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&3 \\ 4&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 7}&2&1 \end{array}} \right| = 2\left\{ {\left( { - 1} \right) - \left( 4 \right)} \right\} + 3\left( {8 - 7} \right) = - 3 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists. {tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&0 \\ 2&1 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 1 - 0} \right) = - 1,{A_{12}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&0 \\ { - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {4 - 0} \right) = - 4{/tex} {tex}{A_{13}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 1} \\ { - 7}&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {8 - 7} \right) = 1,{A_{21}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&1 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {1 - 6} \right) = 5{/tex} {tex}{A_{22}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3 \\ { - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {2 + 21} \right) = 23,{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1 \\ { - 7}&2 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {4 + 7} \right) = 11{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ { - 1}&0 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {0 + 3} \right) = 3,{A_{32}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3 \\ 4&0 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 12} \right) = 12{/tex} {tex}{A_{33}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1 \\ 4&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 2 - 4} \right) = - 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&4&1 \\ 5&{23}&{ - 11} \\ 3&{12}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&4&1 \\ 5&{23}&{ - 11} \\ 3&{12}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A = \frac{{ - 1}}{3} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&5&3 \\ { - 4}&{23}&{12} \\ 1&{ - 11}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&3 \\ 4&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 7}&2&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&3 \\ 4&{ - 1}&0 \\ { - 7}&2&1 \end{array}} \right| = 2\left\{ {\left( { - 1} \right) - \left( 4 \right)} \right\} + 3\left( {8 - 7} \right) = - 3 \ne 0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists.
{tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&0 \\ 2&1 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 1 - 0} \right) = - 1,{A_{12}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&0 \\ { - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {4 - 0} \right) = - 4{/tex}
{tex}{A_{13}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 1} \\ { - 7}&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {8 - 7} \right) = 1,{A_{21}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&1 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {1 - 6} \right) = 5{/tex}
{tex}{A_{22}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3 \\ { - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {2 + 21} \right) = 23,{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1 \\ { - 7}&2 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {4 + 7} \right) = 11{/tex}
{tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ { - 1}&0 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {0 + 3} \right) = 3,{A_{32}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3 \\ 4&0 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 - 12} \right) = 12{/tex}
{tex}{A_{33}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1 \\ 4&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( { - 2 - 4} \right) = - 6{/tex}
{tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&4&1 \\ 5&{23}&{ - 11} \\ 3&{12}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&4&1 \\ 5&{23}&{ - 11} \\ 3&{12}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A = \frac{{ - 1}}{3} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&5&3 \\ { - 4}&{23}&{12} \\ 1&{ - 11}&{ - 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

solution_text: Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 0&2&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 0&2&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right| = 1\left( {8 - 6} \right) - \left( { - 1} \right)\left( {0 + 9} \right) + 2\left( {0 - 6} \right) = - 1 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists. {tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 6} \right) = - 2,{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1} \\ 3&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 2 + 3} \right) = - 1{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 0&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {2 - 0} \right) = 2,{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 9}&{ - 6} \\ 0&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ { - 1}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&0&{ - solution_html:
Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 0&2&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 0&2&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right| = 1\left( {8 - 6} \right) - \left( { - 1} \right)\left( {0 + 9} \right) + 2\left( {0 - 6} \right) = - 1 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}}{/tex} exists.

{tex}{A_{11}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3} \\ { - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {8 - 6} \right) = 2,{A_{12}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 3} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( {0 + 9} \right) = - 9{/tex}

{tex}{A_{13}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2 \\ 3&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {0 - 6} \right) = 6,{A_{21}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&2 \\ { - 2}&4 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 4 + 4} \right) = 0{/tex}

{tex}{A_{22}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&2 \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {4 - 6} \right) = - 2,{A_{23}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1} \\ 3&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 2 + 3} \right) = - 1{/tex}

{tex}{A_{31}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1}&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {3 - 4} \right) = - 1,{A_{32}} = - \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 0&3 \end{array}} \right| = - \left( { - 3 - 0} \right) = 3{/tex}

{tex}{A_{33}} = + \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1} \\ 0&2 \end{array}} \right| = + \left( {2 - 0} \right) = 2,{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adj.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 9}&{ - 6} \\ 0&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ { - 1}&3&2 \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&0&{ - 1} \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \\ { - 6}&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A = \frac{{ - 1}}{1} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&0&{ - 1} \\ { - 9}&{ - 2}&3 \\ { - 6}&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2}&0&1 \\ 9&2&{ - 3} \\ 6&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right| = 15 - 14 = 1 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}adj.A {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Matrix {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right| = 54 - 56 = - 2 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}adj.B = \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 8} \\ { - 7}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&7 \\ 2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&8 \\ 7&9 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18 + 49}&{24 + 63} \\ {12 + 35}&{16 + 45} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {67}&{87} \\ {47}&{61} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| {AB} \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {67}&{87} \\ {47}&{61} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 67(61) - 87(47) = 4087 - 4089 {tex}= - 2 \ne 0{/tex}

Now L.H.S. = {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| {AB} \right|}}adj.\left( {AB} \right) {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {61}&{ - 87} \\ { - 47}&{67} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}….(i)

R.H.S. = B-1A-1 {tex} = \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 8} \\ { - 7}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 7} \\ { - 2}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {45 + 16}&{ - 63 - 24} \\ { - 35 - 12}&{49 + 18} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 2}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {61}&{ - 87} \\ { - 47}&{67} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}….(ii)

{tex}\therefore{/tex} From eq. (i) and (ii), we get

L.H.S. = R.H.S.

{tex} \Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = {B^{ - 1}}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Show that A 2 – 5A + 7I = 0. Hence find A -1
question_html: If {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Show that A2 – 5A + 7I = 0. Hence find A-1
solution_text: We have, {tex} \begin{array}{l}A^2=AA=\begin{bmatrix}3&1\\-1&2\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}3&1\\-1&2\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}8&5\\-5&2\end{bmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} |A| = (3)(2) - (1)(-1) = 6 + 1 = 7 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A is non singular and hence A -1 exists. {tex} {A^2} - 5A + 7I = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&5 \\ { - 5}&2 \end{array}} \right] - 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] + 7\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {8 - 15 + 7}&{5 - 5 + 0} \\ { - 5 + 50}&{3 - 10 + 7} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} = 0 Now, A 2 – 5A + 7I = 0(given) A 2 – 5A = -7I Post multiplying by A -1 , we get, A 2 A -1 -5AA -1 = -7IA -1 AAA -1 – 5AA -1 = -7IA -1 A – 5I = -7A -1 [AA -1 = I] 7A -1 = 5I – A {tex} = 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&-1\\ 1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{7}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex} \begin{array}{l}A^2=AA=\begin{bmatrix}3&1\\-1&2\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}3&1\\-1&2\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}8&5\\-5&2\end{bmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} 
|A| = (3)(2) - (1)(-1) = 6 + 1 = 7 {tex} \neq0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A is non singular and hence A-1 exists.{tex} {A^2} - 5A + 7I = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&5 \\ { - 5}&2 \end{array}} \right] - 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] + 7\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {8 - 15 + 7}&{5 - 5 + 0} \\ { - 5 + 50}&{3 - 10 + 7} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}= 0
Now,
A2 – 5A + 7I = 0(given)
A2 – 5A = -7I
Post multiplying by A-1, we get,
A2A-1 -5AA-1 = -7IA-1
AAA-1 – 5AA-1 = -7IA-1
A – 5I = -7A-1 [AA-1 = I]
7A-1 = 5I – A
{tex} = 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1 \\ { - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&-1\\ 1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{7}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_text: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , find the numbers a and b such that A 2 + aA + bI = 0.
question_html: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, find the numbers a and b such that A2 + aA + bI = 0.
solution_text: Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^2} = A.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 + 2}&{6 + 2} \\ {3 + 1}&{2 + 1} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^2} + aA + b{I_2} = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right] + a\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right] + b\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {3a}&{2a} \\ a&a \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} b&0 \\ 0&b \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11 + 3a + b}&{8 + 2a + 0} \\ {4 + a + 0}&{3 + a + b} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} We have {tex}11 + 3a + b = 0{/tex} ….(i) {tex}8 + 2a + 0 = 0{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}\Rightarrow 2a = - 8{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a = - 4{/tex} Here a = -4 satisfies 4 + a + 0 = 0 also, therefore a = -4 Putting a = -4 in eq. (i), 11 - 12 + b = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow b - 1 = 0 \Rightarrow b = 1{/tex} Here also b = 1 satisfies 3 + a + b = 0, therefore b = 1 Therefore, a = - 4 and b = 1
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = A.A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 + 2}&{6 + 2} \\ {3 + 1}&{2 + 1} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} + aA + b{I_2} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right] + a\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2 \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right] + b\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&8 \\ 4&3 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {3a}&{2a} \\ a&a \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} b&0 \\ 0&b \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11 + 3a + b}&{8 + 2a + 0} \\ {4 + a + 0}&{3 + a + b} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0 \\ 0&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} We have {tex}11 + 3a + b = 0{/tex}….(i)

{tex}8 + 2a + 0 = 0{/tex} ...(ii)

{tex}\Rightarrow 2a = - 8{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow a = - 4{/tex}

Here a = -4 satisfies 4 + a + 0 = 0 also, therefore a = -4

Putting a = -4 in eq. (i), 11 - 12 + b = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow b - 1 = 0 \Rightarrow b = 1{/tex}

Here also b = 1 satisfies 3 + a + b = 0, therefore b = 1

Therefore, a = - 4 and b = 1

question_text: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , show that A 3 - 6A 2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A -1 .
question_html: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, show that A3 - 6A2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A-1.
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 1 + 2}&{1 + 2 - 1}&{1 - 3 + 3} \\ {1 + 2 - 6}&{1 + 4 + 3}&{1 - 6 - 9} \\ {2 - 1 + 6}&{2 - 2 - 3}&{2 + 3 + 9} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now {tex}{A^3} = {A^2}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {4 + 2 + 2}&{4 + 4 + 1}&{4 - 6 + 3} \\ { - 3 + 8 - 28}&{ - 3 + 16 + 14}&{ - 3 - 24 - 42} \\ {7 - 3 + 28}&{7 - 6 - 14}&{7 + 9 + 42} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

L.H.S. {tex} = {A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right] + {/tex}{tex}5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] + 11\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {24}&{12}&6 \\ { - 18}&{48}&{ - 84} \\ {42}&{ - 18}&{84} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&5&5 \\ 5&{10}&{ - 15} \\ {10}&{ - 5}&{15} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {8 - 24 + 5}&{7 - 12 + 5}&{1 - 6 + 5} \\ { - 23 + 18 + 5}&{27 - 48 + 10}&{ - 69 + 84 - 15} \\ {32 - 42 + 10}&{ - 13 + 18 - 5}&{58 + 84 + 15} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 11}&0&0 \\ 0&{ - 11}&0 \\ 0&0&{ - 11} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}= R.H.S.

Now,   to find {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}, multiplying {tex}{A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I = 0{/tex} by {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^3}{A^{ - 1}} - 6{A^2}{A^{ - 1}} + 5A{A^{ - 1}} + 11I.{A^{ - 1}} = 0.{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} - 6A + 5I + 11{A^{ - 1}} = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6A - 5I - {A^2}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&6&6 \\ 6&{12}&{ - 18} \\ {12}&{ - 6}&{18} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&0&0 \\ 0&5&0 \\ 0&0&5 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 5 - 4}&{6 - 2}&{6 - 1} \\ {6 + 3}&{12 - 5 - 8}&{ - 18 + 14} \\ {12 - 7}&{ - 6 + 3}&{18 - 5 - 14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , verify that A 3 - 6A 2 + 9A - 4I = 0 and hence find A -1
question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, verify that A3 - 6A2 + 9A - 4I = 0 and hence find A-1
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&-1&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&-1&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^2}=\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {4 + 1 + 1}&{ - 2 - 2 - 1}&{2 + 1 + 2} \\ { - 2 - 2 - 1}&{1 + 4 + 1}&{ - 1 - 2 - 2} \\ {2 + 1 + 2}&{ - 1 - 2 - 2}&{1 + 1 + 4} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now A3 = A2.A  {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12 + 5 + 5}&{ - 6 - 10 - 5}&{6 + 5 + 10} \\ { - 10 - 6 - 5}&{5 + 12 + 5}&{ - 5 - 6 - 10} \\ {10 + 5 + 6}&{ - 5 - 10 - 6}&{5 + 5 + 12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

L.H.S. {tex} = {A^3} - 6{A^2} + 9A - 4I{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right] - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ 9\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] - 4\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22}&{ - 21}&{21} \\ { - 21}&{22}&{ - 21} \\ {21}&{ - 21}&{22} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {36}&{ - 30}&{30} \\ { - 30}&{36}&{ - 30} \\ {30}&{ - 30}&{36} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18}&{ - 9}&9 \\ { - 9}&{18}&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 9}&{18} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&0&0 \\ 0&4&0 \\ 0&0&4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {22 - 36}&{ - 21 + 30}&{21 - 30} \\ { - 21 + 30}&{22 - 36}&{ - 21 + 30} \\ {21 - 30}&{ - 21 + 30}&{22 - 36} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {18 - 4}&{ - 9 - 0}&{9 - 0} \\ { - 9 - 0}&{18 - 4}&{ - 9 - 0} \\ {9 - 0}&{ - 9 - 0}&{18 - 4} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 14}&9&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 14}&9 \\ { - 9}&9&{14} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 14}&9&{ - 9} \\ 9&{ - 14}&9 \\ { - 9}&9&{14} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}= R.H.S.

Now,   to find {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}, multiplying {tex}{A^3} - 6{A^2} + 9A - 4{I^{ - 1}} = 0.{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}by {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^3}{A^{ - 1}} - 6{A^2}{A^{ - 1}} + 9A{A^{ - 1}} - 4I.{A^{ - 1}} = 0{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} - 6A + 9I - 4{A^{ - 1}} = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A}^{-1} = {A^2} - 6A + 9I{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A}^{-1} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 1}&2&{ - 1} \\ 1&{ - 1}&2 \end{array}} \right] + 9\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 5}&5 \\ { - 5}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 5}&6 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12}&{ - 6}&6 \\ { - 6}&{12}&{ - 6} \\ 6&{ - 6}&{12} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&0&0 \\ 0&9&0 \\ 0&0&9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 4{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 12 + 9}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0}&{5 - 6 + 0} \\ { - 5 + 6 + 0}&{6 - 12 + 9}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0} \\ {5 - 6 + 0}&{ - 5 + 6 + 0}&{6 - 12 + 9} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&{ - 1} \\ 1&3&1 \\ { - 1}&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&{ - 1} \\ 1&3&1 \\ { - 1}&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_text: Let A be a non-singular square matrix of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3. Then |adj A| is equal to
question_html: Let A be a non-singular square matrix of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3. Then |adj A| is equal to

options.explanation: For a square matrix of order n {tex}\times{/tex} n, We know that {tex}A.adjA=|A|I{/tex} Here, n=3 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}|A.adjA|=|A|^n{/tex} {tex}|adjA|=|A|^{n-1}{/tex} So, {tex}|AdjA| = |A|^{3-1} = |A|^2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: If A is an invertible matrix of order 2, then det (A –1 ) is equal to
question_html: If A is an invertible matrix of order 2, then det (A–1) is equal to

options.explanation: We know that, A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{A}|}{/tex} Adj (A) So, {tex}\left|A^{-1}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{Adj}(A)\right|{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|^{n}}|\operatorname{Adj}(A)|{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|^{n}}|A|^{n-1}=\frac{1}{|A|^{1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{|A|^{1}}{/tex} {since adj(A) is of order n and |Adj(A)| = |A| n-1 }

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation x + 2y = 2; 2x + 3y = 3
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation x + 2y = 2; 2x + 3y = 3
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right| = 3 - 4 = - 1 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2 \\ 2&3 \end{array}} \right| = 3 - 4 = - 1 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}2x - y = 5;\,\,x + y = 4{/tex}
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}2x - y = 5;\,\,x + y = 4{/tex}
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right| = 2 - \left( { - 1} \right) = 3 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, Unique solution and hence, equations are consistent.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 1&1 \end{array}} \right| = 2 - \left( { - 1} \right) = 3 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, Unique solution and hence, equations are consistent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}x + 3y = 5;\,\,2x + 6y = 8\,{/tex}
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}x + 3y = 5;\,\,2x + 6y = 8\,{/tex}
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 6 – 6 = 0 Now {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right)B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 3} \\ { - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {30 - 24} \\ { - 10 + 8} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ { - 2} \end{array}} \right] \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, given equations are inconsistent, i.e., have no common solution.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&3 \\ 2&6 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 6 – 6 = 0

Now {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right)B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&{ - 3} \\ { - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {30 - 24} \\ { - 10 + 8} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ { - 2} \end{array}} \right] \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, given equations are inconsistent, i.e., have no common solution.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation x + y + z = 1; 2x + 3y + 2z = 2; ax + ay + 2az = 4
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation x + y + z = 1; 2x + 3y + 2z = 2; ax + ay + 2az = 4
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 1(6a - 2a) - 1(4a - 2a) + 1(2a - 3a) {tex} = 4a - 2a - a \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Here, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 2&3&2 \\ a&a&{2a} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 1(6a - 2a) - 1(4a - 2a) + 1(2a - 3a) {tex} = 4a - 2a - a \ne 0{/tex}
Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}3x - y - 2z = 2;\,\,2y - z = - 1;3x - 5y = 3{/tex}
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation {tex}3x - y - 2z = 2;\,\,2y - z = - 1;3x - 5y = 3{/tex}
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&5&0 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&5&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&{ - 5}&0 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left| A \right| = 3\left( {0 - 5} \right) - \left( { - 1} \right)\left( {0 + 3} \right) + \left( { - 2} \right)\left( {0 - 6} \right){/tex} {tex}= 3\left( { - 5} \right) + 3 + 12 = - 15 + 15 = 0{/tex} Now {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right) = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&{10}&5 \\ { - 3}&6&3 \\ { - 6}&{12}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} And {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right)B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&{10}&5 \\ { - 3}&6&3 \\ { - 6}&{12}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 10 - 10 + 15} \\ { - 6 - 6 + 9} \\ { - 12 - 12 + 18} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5} \\ { - 3} \\ { - 6} \end{array}} \right] \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, given equations are inconsistent.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&5&0 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&5&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&2&{ - 1} \\ 3&{ - 5}&0 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left| A \right| = 3\left( {0 - 5} \right) - \left( { - 1} \right)\left( {0 + 3} \right) + \left( { - 2} \right)\left( {0 - 6} \right){/tex}{tex}= 3\left( { - 5} \right) + 3 + 12 = - 15 + 15 = 0{/tex}

Now {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right) = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&{10}&5 \\ { - 3}&6&3 \\ { - 6}&{12}&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

And {tex}\left( {adj.A} \right)B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&{10}&5 \\ { - 3}&6&3 \\ { - 6}&{12}&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 10 - 10 + 15} \\ { - 6 - 6 + 9} \\ { - 12 - 12 + 18} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5} \\ { - 3} \\ { - 6} \end{array}} \right] \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, given equations are inconsistent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Examine the consistency of the system of equation 5x - y + 4z = 5; 2x + 3y + 5z = 2; 5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
question_html: Examine the consistency of the system of equation 5x - y + 4z = 5; 2x + 3y + 5z = 2; 5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} |A| = 5(18 + 10) - (-1)(12 - 25) + 4(-4 - 15) = 140 - 13 - 76 = 140 - 89 {tex} = 51 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&{ - 1}&4 \\ 2&3&5 \\ 5&-2&6 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} |A| = 5(18 + 10) - (-1)(12 - 25) + 4(-4 - 15) = 140 - 13 - 76 = 140 - 89 {tex} = 51 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, Unique solution and hence equations are consistent.

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 5x + 2y = 4; 7x + 3y = 5
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 5x + 2y = 4; 7x + 3y = 5
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right| = 15 - 14 = 1 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 2} \\ { - 7}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12 - 10} \\ { - 28 + 25} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 2 and y = - 3
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 7&3 \end{array}} \right| = 15 - 14 = 1 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 2} \\ { - 7}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {12 - 10} \\ { - 28 + 25} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, x = 2 and y = - 3

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x - y = - 2; 3x + 4y = 3
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x - y = - 2; 3x + 4y = 3
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 8 - (-3) = 8 + 3 {tex}= 11 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&1 \\ { - 3}&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8 + 3} \\ {6 + 6} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{ - 5}}{{11}}} \\ {\frac{{12}}{{11}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, {tex}x = \frac{{ - 5}}{{11}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{12}}{{11}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1} \\ 3&4 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 8 - (-3) = 8 + 3 {tex}= 11 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&1 \\ { - 3}&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 8 + 3} \\ {6 + 6} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{ - 5}}{{11}}} \\ {\frac{{12}}{{11}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, {tex}x = \frac{{ - 5}}{{11}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{12}}{{11}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 4x - 3y = 3; 3x - 5y = 7
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 4x - 3y = 3; 3x - 5y = 7
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = - 20 - (-9) = - 20 + 9 = - 11 {tex} \ne {/tex} 0 Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&3 \\ { - 3}&4 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 15 + 21} \\ { - 9 + 28} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{ - 6}}{{11}}} \\ {\frac{{ - 19}}{{11}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, {tex}x = \frac{{ - 6}}{{11}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{ - 19}}{{11}}{/tex}
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&{ - 3} \\ 3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = - 20 - (-9) = - 20 + 9 = - 11 {tex} \ne {/tex} 0

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 5}&3 \\ { - 3}&4 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 15 + 21} \\ { - 9 + 28} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{ - 11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6 \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{ - 6}}{{11}}} \\ {\frac{{ - 19}}{{11}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, {tex}x = \frac{{ - 6}}{{11}}{/tex} and {tex}y = \frac{{ - 19}}{{11}}{/tex}

question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 5x + 2y = 3; 3x + 2y = 5
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 5x + 2y = 3; 3x + 2y = 5
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&2 \\ 3&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 2 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 2 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 10 - 6 = 4 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 Therefore, solution is unique and X = A -1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} (adj A) B {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&-2 \\ { - 3}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 10} \\ { - 9 + 25} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4} \\ {16} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = -1 and y = 4

Here A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 2 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 2 \\ 3 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 10 - 6 = 4 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0
Therefore, solution is unique and X = A-1B = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} (adj A) B
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&-2 \\ { - 3}&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ 5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 10} \\ { - 9 + 25} \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4} \\ {16} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 1} \\ 4 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Therefore, x = -1 and y = 4

question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + y + z = 1; {tex}x - 2y - z = \frac{3}{2};\,\,3y - 5z = 9{/tex}
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + y + z = 1; {tex}x - 2y - z = \frac{3}{2};\,\,3y - 5z = 9{/tex}
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here, {tex}A_{11}=13,A_{12}=5,A_{13}=3\\ A_{21}=8,A_{22}=-10,A_{23}=-6\\ A_{31}=1,A_{32}=3,A_{33}=-5{/tex} adj A= {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 13&8&1 \\ 5&{ - 10}&{ 3} \\ 3&-6&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 2(10 + 3) - 1(3 - 0) = 26 + 5 + 3 {tex} = 34 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {13}&8&1 \\ 5&{ - 10}&3 \\ 3&{ - 6}&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {13 + 12 + 9} \\ {5 - 15 + 27} \\ {3 - 9 - 45} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {34} \\ {17} \\ { - 51} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, {tex}x = 1,y = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}z = \frac{-3}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}  

Here,{tex}A_{11}=13,A_{12}=5,A_{13}=3\\ A_{21}=8,A_{22}=-10,A_{23}=-6\\ A_{31}=1,A_{32}=3,A_{33}=-5{/tex}

adj A={tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 13&8&1 \\ 5&{ - 10}&{ 3} \\ 3&-6&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&1&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 1} \\ 0&3&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = 2(10 + 3) - 1(3 - 0)  = 26 + 5 + 3 {tex} = 34 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {13}&8&1 \\ 5&{ - 10}&3 \\ 3&{ - 6}&{ - 5} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{3}{2}} \\ 9 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {13 + 12 + 9} \\ {5 - 15 + 27} \\ {3 - 9 - 45} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{{34}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {34} \\ {17} \\ { - 51} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, {tex}x = 1,y = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}z = \frac{-3}{2}{/tex}

question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method x - y + z = 4; 2x + y - 3z = 0; x + y + z = 2
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method x - y + z = 4; 2x + y - 3z = 0; x + y + z = 2
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(1 + 3) - ( - 1)(2 + 3) + 1(2 - 1) {tex} = 4 + 5 + 1 = 10 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&2 \\ { - 5}&0&5 \\ 1&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {16 + 0 + 4} \\ { - 20 + 0 + 10} \\ {4 +0 + 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {20} \\ { - 10} \\ {10} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 2, y = - 1 and z = 1
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 2&1&{ - 3} \\ 1&1&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

 = 1(1 + 3) - ( - 1)(2 + 3) + 1(2 - 1)

{tex} = 4 + 5 + 1 = 10 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&2 \\ { - 5}&0&5 \\ 1&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 0 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {16 + 0 + 4} \\ { - 20 + 0 + 10} \\ {4 +0 + 6} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{10}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {20} \\ { - 10} \\ {10} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ { - 1} \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, x = 2, y = - 1 and z = 1

question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + 3y + 3z = 5; x - 2y + z = - 4; 3x - y - 2z = 3
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + 3y + 3z = 5; x - 2y + z = - 4; 3x - y - 2z = 3
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 2(4 + 1) - 3( - 2 - 3) + 3( - 1 + 6) {tex} = 10 + 15 + 15 = 40 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&3&9 \\ 5&{ - 13}&1 \\ 5&{11}&{ - 7} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {25 - 12 + 27} \\ {25 + 52 + 3} \\ {25 - 44 - 21} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {40} \\ {80} \\ { - 40} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = - 1 .
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 2(4 + 1) - 3( - 2 - 3) + 3( - 1 + 6)

{tex} = 10 + 15 + 15 = 40 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&3&9 \\ 5&{ - 13}&1 \\ 5&{11}&{ - 7} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {25 - 12 + 27} \\ {25 + 52 + 3} \\ {25 - 44 - 21} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {40} \\ {80} \\ { - 40} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = - 1 .

question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method x - y + 2z = 7; 3x + 4y - 5z = -5; 2x - y + 3z = 12
question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method x - y + 2z = 7; 3x + 4y - 5z = -5; 2x - y + 3z = 12
solution_text: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(12 - 5) - (-1)(9 + 10) + 2(-3 - 8) = 7 + 19 - 22 = 4 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 A 11 = 7, A 12 = -19, A 13 = -11 A 21 =1, A 22 = -1, A 23 = -1 A 31 = -3, A 32 =11, A 33 = 7 adj A= {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&1&{ - 3} \\ { - 19}&{ - 1}&{11} \\ { - 11}&{ - 1}&7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&1&{ - 3} \\ { - 19}&{ - 1}&{11} \\ { - 11}&{ - 1}&7 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {49 - 5 - 36} \\ { - 133 + 5 + 132} \\ { - 77 + 5 + 84} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8 \\ 4 \\ {12} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right],X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&2 \\ 3&4&{ - 5} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 1(12 - 5) - (-1)(9 + 10) + 2(-3 - 8)

= 7 + 19 - 22 = 4 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0

A11 = 7, A12 = -19, A13 = -11
A21 =1, A22 = -1, A23 = -1
A31 = -3, A32 =11, A33 = 7

adj A={tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&1&{ - 3} \\ { - 19}&{ - 1}&{11} \\ { - 11}&{ - 1}&7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7&1&{ - 3} \\ { - 19}&{ - 1}&{11} \\ { - 11}&{ - 1}&7 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 7 \\ { - 5} \\ {12} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {49 - 5 - 36} \\ { - 133 + 5 + 132} \\ { - 77 + 5 + 84} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8 \\ 4 \\ {12} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3
