question_text: If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A -1 , using A -1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 x + y - 2z = -3
question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A-1, using A-1 solve the system of equations
2x - 3y + 5z = 11
3x + 2y - 4z = -5
x + y - 2z = -3
solution_text: A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1 Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23,A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ....(1) Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ] {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1
Now, A11 = 0, A12= 2, A13= 1
A21= -1, A22 = -9, A23= -5
A31= 2, A32= 23,A33 = 13

 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}....(1)

Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A-1B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ]

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_text: The cost of 4kg onion, 3kg wheat and 2kg rice is Rs. 60. The cost of 2kg onion, 4kg wheat and 6kg rice is Rs. 90. The cost of 6kg onion 2kg wheat and 3kg rice is Rs. 70. Find the cost of each item per kg by matrix method.
question_html: The cost of 4kg onion, 3kg wheat and 2kg rice is Rs. 60. The cost of 2kg onion, 4kg wheat and 6kg rice is Rs. 90. The cost of 6kg onion 2kg wheat and 3kg rice is Rs. 70. Find the cost of each item per kg by matrix method.
solution_text: Let cost of 1kg onion = x cost of 1kg wheat = y cost of 1kg rise = z By the question ,we have, 4x + 3y + 2z = 60 2x + 4y + 6z = 90 6x + 2y + 3z = 70 {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {60} \\ {90} \\ {70} \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left| A \right| = \left|{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right|= 50 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}Now,A_{11}=0,A_{12}=30,A_{13}=-20{/tex} {tex}A_{21}=-5,A_{22}=0,A_{23}=10{/tex} {tex}A_{31}=10,A_{32}=-20,A_{33}=10{/tex} {tex}\therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{50}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 5, y = 8, z = 8
solution_html:
Let  cost of 1kg onion = x

cost of 1kg wheat = y

cost of 1kg rise = z

By the question ,we have,

4x + 3y + 2z = 60

2x + 4y + 6z = 90

6x + 2y + 3z = 70

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right]B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {60} \\ {90} \\ {70} \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left| A \right| = \left|{\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&3&2 \\ 2&4&6 \\ 6&2&3 \end{array}} \right|= 50 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}Now,A_{11}=0,A_{12}=30,A_{13}=-20{/tex}

{tex}A_{21}=-5,A_{22}=0,A_{23}=10{/tex}

{tex}A_{31}=10,A_{32}=-20,A_{33}=10{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{50}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 5}&{10} \\ {30}&0&{ - 20} \\ { - 20}&{10}&{10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ 8 \\ 8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

x = 5, y = 8, z = 8

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex} .
question_html: Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}.
solution_text: Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x 3 - x + x = - x 3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

Expanding along first row,

{tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x3 - x + x = - x3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha \cos \beta }&{\cos \alpha \sin \beta }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ { - \sin \beta }&{\cos \beta }&0 \\ {\sin \alpha \cos \beta }&{\sin \alpha \sin \beta }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate: {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha \cos \beta }&{\cos \alpha \sin \beta }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ { - \sin \beta }&{\cos \beta }&0 \\ {\sin \alpha \cos \beta }&{\sin \alpha \sin \beta }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha \cos \beta }&{\cos \alpha \sin \beta }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ { - \sin \beta }&{\cos \beta }&0 \\ {\sin \alpha \cos \beta }&{\sin \alpha \sin \beta }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right|{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}= \cos \alpha \cos \beta \left( {\cos \alpha \cos \beta - 0} \right) {/tex} {tex}- \cos \alpha \sin \beta \left( { - \cos \alpha \sin \beta - 0} \right) {/tex} {tex}- \sin \alpha \left( { - \sin \alpha {{\sin }^2}\beta - \sin \alpha {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex} {tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha {\cos ^2}\beta + {\cos ^2}\alpha {\sin ^2}\beta {/tex} {tex}+ {\sin ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\sin }^2}\beta + {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex} {tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\cos }^2}\beta + {{\sin }^2}\beta } \right){/tex} {tex} + {\sin ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\sin }^2}\beta + {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex} {tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha + {\sin ^2}\alpha{/tex} {tex}={/tex} 1
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha \cos \beta }&{\cos \alpha \sin \beta }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ { - \sin \beta }&{\cos \beta }&0 \\ {\sin \alpha \cos \beta }&{\sin \alpha \sin \beta }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

Expanding along first row,

{tex}= \cos \alpha \cos \beta \left( {\cos \alpha \cos \beta - 0} \right) {/tex} {tex}- \cos \alpha \sin \beta \left( { - \cos \alpha \sin \beta - 0} \right) {/tex} {tex}- \sin \alpha \left( { - \sin \alpha {{\sin }^2}\beta - \sin \alpha {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex}

{tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha {\cos ^2}\beta + {\cos ^2}\alpha {\sin ^2}\beta {/tex} {tex}+ {\sin ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\sin }^2}\beta + {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex}

{tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\cos }^2}\beta + {{\sin }^2}\beta } \right){/tex} {tex} + {\sin ^2}\alpha \left( {{{\sin }^2}\beta + {{\cos }^2}\beta } \right){/tex}

{tex}= {\cos ^2}\alpha + {\sin ^2}\alpha{/tex}

{tex}={/tex} 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}find (AB)-1
solution_text: Given: {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}{B^{ }} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Since, (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 [Reversal law] .....(i) Now {tex}\left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(3 - 0) - 2(-1 - 0) + (-2)(2 - 0) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 = {tex}\ne {/tex} 0 Therefore, B -1 exists. {tex}\therefore {/tex} B 11 = 3, B 12 = 1, B 13 = 2 and B 21 = 2, B 22 = 1, B 23 = 2 and B 31 = 6, B 22 = 2, B 33 = 5 {tex}\therefore adj.B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&2 \\ 2&1&2 \\ 6&2&5 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}\left( {adj.B} \right) = \frac{1}{1} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 - 30 + 30}&{ - 3 + 12 - 12}&{3 - 10 + 12} \\ {3 - 15 + 10}&{ - 1 + 6 - 4}&{1 - 5 + 4} \\ {6 - 30 + 25}&{ - 2 + 12 - 10}&{2 - 10 + 10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 3}&5 \\ { - 2}&1&0 \\ 1&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}{B^{ }} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Since, (AB)-1 = B-1A-1 [Reversal law] .....(i)

Now {tex}\left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 1(3 - 0) - 2(-1 - 0) + (-2)(2 - 0) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 = {tex}\ne {/tex} 0

Therefore, B-1 exists.

{tex}\therefore {/tex} B11 = 3, B12 = 1, B13 = 2 and B21 = 2, B22 = 1, B23 = 2 and B31 = 6, B22 = 2, B33 = 5

{tex}\therefore adj.B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&2 \\ 2&1&2 \\ 6&2&5 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}\left( {adj.B} \right) = \frac{1}{1} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

From eq. (i), {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 - 30 + 30}&{ - 3 + 12 - 12}&{3 - 10 + 12} \\ {3 - 15 + 10}&{ - 1 + 6 - 4}&{1 - 5 + 4} \\ {6 - 30 + 25}&{ - 2 + 12 - 10}&{2 - 10 + 10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 3}&5 \\ { - 2}&1&0 \\ 1&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} . verify that [adj A] –1 = adj (A –1 )
question_html: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}. verify that [adj A]–1 = adj (A–1)
solution_html:
Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-2} & {1} \\ {-2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} |A|=1(15-1) + 2(-10-1) + 1(-2-3) = 14-22-5 = -13
Now, {tex}\begin{aligned} &A_{11}=14, A_{12}=11, A_{13}=-5\\ &A_{21}=11, A_{22}=4, A_{23}=-3\\ &A_{31}=-5, A_{12}=-3, A_{13}=-1 \end{aligned}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}a d j A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
So, we have {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|}(a d j A){/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Clearly, {tex}a d j A={/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj A| = 14(-4-9)-11(-11-15)-5(-33+20)
= 14(-13)-11(-26)-5(-13)
= -182 + 286 + 65 = 169
we have,
adj (adj A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-13} & {26} & {-13} \\ {26} & {-39} & {-13} \\ {-13} & {-13} & {-65} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}[\operatorname{adj} A]^{\prime}=\frac{1}{|a d j A|}(\operatorname{adj}(\operatorname{adj} A)){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{169}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-13} & {26} & {-13} \\ {26} & {-39} & {-13} \\ {-13} & {-13} & {-65} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-\frac{14}{13}} & {-\frac{11}{13}} & {\frac{5}{13}} \\ {-\frac{11}{13}} & {-\frac{4}{13}} & {\frac{3}{13}} \\ {\frac{5}{13}} & {\frac{3}{13}} & {\frac{1}{13}} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} adj (A-1) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-\frac{4}{169}-\frac{9}{169}} & {-\left(-\frac{11}{169}-\frac{15}{169}\right)} & {-\frac{33}{169}+\frac{20}{169}} \\ {-\left(-\frac{11}{169}-\frac{15}{169}\right)} & {-\frac{14}{169}-\frac{25}{169}} & {-\left(-\frac{42}{169}+\frac{55}{169}\right)} \\ {-\frac{33}{169}+\frac{20}{169}} & {-\left(-\frac{42}{169}+\frac{55}{169}\right)} & {\frac{56}{169}-\frac{121}{169}} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{169}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-13} & {26} & {-13} \\ {26} & {-39} & {-13} \\ {-13} & {-13} & {-65} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, [adj A]-1 = adj(A-1)

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} . verify that (A –1 ) –1 = A
question_html: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}. verify that (A–1)–1 = A
solution_text: Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-2} & {1} \\ {-2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} |A|=1(15-1) + 2(-10-1) + 1(-2-3) = 14-22-5 = -13 Now, {tex}\begin{aligned} &A_{11}=14, A_{12}=11, A_{13}=-5\\ &A_{21}=11, A_{22}=4, A_{23}=-3\\ &A_{31}=-5, A_{12}=-3, A_{13}=-1 \end{aligned}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}a d j A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|}(a d j A){/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} We have shown that {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and, Adj A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now {tex}\left|A^{-1}\right|=\left(\frac{1}{13}\right)^{3}[-14 \times(-13)+11 \times(-26)+5 \times(-13)]=\left(\frac{1}{13}\right)^{3} \times(-169)=-\frac{1}{13}{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}\left(A^{-1}\right)^{-1}=\frac{a d j A^{-1}}{\left|A^{-1}\right|}=\frac{1}{\left(-\frac{1}{13}\right)} \times \frac{1}{13}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-2} & {1} \\ {-2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]=A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (A -1 ) -1 = A
solution_html:
Let {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-2} & {1} \\ {-2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} |A|=1(15-1) + 2(-10-1) + 1(-2-3) = 14-22-5 = -13
Now, {tex}\begin{aligned} &A_{11}=14, A_{12}=11, A_{13}=-5\\ &A_{21}=11, A_{22}=4, A_{23}=-3\\ &A_{31}=-5, A_{12}=-3, A_{13}=-1 \end{aligned}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}a d j A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|}(a d j A){/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {14} & {11} & {-5} \\ {11} & {4} & {-3} \\ {-5} & {-3} & {-1} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
We have shown that
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-14} & {-11} & {5} \\ {-11} & {-4} & {3} \\ {5} & {3} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
and, Adj A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{13}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now
{tex}\left|A^{-1}\right|=\left(\frac{1}{13}\right)^{3}[-14 \times(-13)+11 \times(-26)+5 \times(-13)]=\left(\frac{1}{13}\right)^{3} \times(-169)=-\frac{1}{13}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} {tex}\left(A^{-1}\right)^{-1}=\frac{a d j A^{-1}}{\left|A^{-1}\right|}=\frac{1}{\left(-\frac{1}{13}\right)} \times \frac{1}{13}{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {2} & {-3} & {-1} \\ {-1} & {-1} & {-5} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-2} & {1} \\ {-2} & {3} & {1} \\ {1} & {1} & {5} \end{array}\right]=A{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (A-1)-1 = A

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate: {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&y&{x + y} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x+ y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate: {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&y&{x + y} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x+ y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&y&{x + y} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\left[ {{R_1} \to {R_1} + {R_2} + {R_3}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2\left( {x + y} \right)}&{2\left( {x + y} \right)}&{2\left( {x + y} \right)} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex} Taking 2(x+y) common from first row {tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\left[ {{C_2} \to {C_2} - {C_1}and\,\,{C_3} \to {C_3} - {C_1}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ y&{x + y - y}&{x - y} \\ {x + y}&{x - x - y}&{y - x - y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ y&x&{x - y} \\ {x + y}&{ - y}&{ - x} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} Expanding along Ist row {tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right).1\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{x - y} \\ { - y}&{ - x} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 2(x + y){ -x 2 + y(x - y)} = 2(x + y)(-x 2 + xy - y 2 ) = -2(x + y)(x 2 - xy + y 2 ) = -2(x 3 + y 3 )
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&y&{x + y} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {{R_1} \to {R_1} + {R_2} + {R_3}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {2\left( {x + y} \right)}&{2\left( {x + y} \right)}&{2\left( {x + y} \right)} \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

Taking 2(x+y) common from first row

{tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ y&{x + y}&x \\ {x + y}&x&y \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {{C_2} \to {C_2} - {C_1}and\,\,{C_3} \to {C_3} - {C_1}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ y&{x + y - y}&{x - y} \\ {x + y}&{x - x - y}&{y - x - y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right)\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ y&x&{x - y} \\ {x + y}&{ - y}&{ - x} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

Expanding along Ist row

{tex}= 2\left( {x + y} \right).1\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{x - y} \\ { - y}&{ - x} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 2(x + y){ -x2 + y(x - y)}

= 2(x + y)(-x2 + xy - y2)

= -2(x + y)(x2 - xy + y2)

= -2(x3 + y3)

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Evaluate : {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 1&{x + y}&y \\ 1&x&{x + y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
question_html: Evaluate : {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 1&{x + y}&y \\ 1&x&{x + y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 1&{x + y}&y \\ 1&x&{x + y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\left[ {{R_2} \to {R_2} - {R_1}\,\,and\,\,{R_3} \to {R_3} - {R_1}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 0&{x + y - y}&0 \\ 0&0&{x + y - y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 0&y&0 \\ 0&0&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} Expanding along Ist column {tex}= 1\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y&0 \\ 0&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = xy
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 1&{x + y}&y \\ 1&x&{x + y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {{R_2} \to {R_2} - {R_1}\,\,and\,\,{R_3} \to {R_3} - {R_1}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 0&{x + y - y}&0 \\ 0&0&{x + y - y} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&x&y \\ 0&y&0 \\ 0&0&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

Expanding along Ist column

{tex}= 1\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y&0 \\ 0&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= xy

subject: Mathematics
question_text: Solve the system of equations {tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex} {tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex} {tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
question_html:
Solve the system of equations
{tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex}
{tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex}
{tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
solution_text: Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} 2u + 3v + 10w = 4 4u – 6v+ 5w = 1 6u + 9v – 20w = 2 {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36] = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A is non-singular and hence A -1 exists. Now, A 11 = 75, A 12 = 110, A 13 = 72 A 21 = 150, A 22 = -100, A 23 = 0 A 31 = 75, A 32 = 30, A 33 = -2 {tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
solution_html:
Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex}
2u + 3v + 10w = 4
4u – 6v+ 5w = 1
6u + 9v – 20w = 2
{tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex}

= 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36]

= 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200{tex} \neq0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex}A is non-singular and hence A-1 exists.

Now, A11 = 75, A12 = 110, A13 = 72
A21 = 150, A22 = -100, A23 = 0
A31 = 75, A32 = 30, A33 = -2

{tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

{tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

x = 2, y = 3, z = 5

options.explanation: Here, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {x} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {y} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Clearly, we can see that {tex}adjA{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {yz} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {xz} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {xy} \end{array}\right]~~and ~~~|A|=xyz{/tex} {tex}\therefore ~A^{-1}=\frac {adjA}{|A|}=\frac1{xyz}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {{yz}} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {{xz}} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {xy} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {x^{-1}} & {0} & {0} \\ {0} & {y^{-1}} & {0} \\ {0} & {0} & {z^{-1}} \end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_text: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {\sin \theta} & {1} \\ {-\sin \theta} & {1} & {\sin \theta} \\ {-1} & {-\sin \theta} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} where {tex}0 \leq \theta \leq 2 \pi{/tex} . Then

options.explanation: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {\sin \theta} & {1} \\ {-\sin \theta} & {1} & {\sin \theta} \\ {-1} & {-\sin \theta} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} |A| = 1 (1 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 – sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (-sin {tex}\theta{/tex} )) – sin {tex}\theta{/tex} (-sin {tex}\theta{/tex} + {tex}sin\theta{/tex} ) + 1 [(- sin {tex}\theta)~\times (-sin \theta )-(-1)\times1]{/tex} |A| = {tex}1+sin^2\theta+sin^2\theta +1{/tex} |A| = {tex}2 + 2~sin^2\theta{/tex} |A| = {tex}2(1 + sin2\theta){/tex} Now, 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\leq{/tex} 2 {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} sin {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\leq{/tex} sin 2 {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\leq{/tex} 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 + 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} 1 + sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} {tex}\leq{/tex} 1 + 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 2(1 + sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} ) {tex}\leq{/tex} 4 {tex}\therefore{/tex} Det (A) {tex}\in{/tex} [2, 4]
